sk.q: Commitol si predčasne. Chceš zrušiť posledný commit, ale ponechať zmeny na úpravu. Čo použiješ?
options: git reset --soft HEAD~1
git reset --hard HEAD~1
git push --force origin master
git restore --staged . a potom commitneš znova
sk.explain: --soft posunie HEAD o commit späť, ale stage aj working tree nechá. --hard by zmeny zahodil.

en.q: You committed too early. You want to undo the last commit but keep the changes to edit. What do you use?
options: git reset --soft HEAD~1
git reset --hard HEAD~1
git push --force origin master
git restore --staged . and then commit again
en.explain: --soft moves HEAD back one commit but keeps the stage and working tree. --hard would discard the changes.

sk.q: V jednom súbore si narobil zmeny, ktoré chceš úplne zahodiť (späť na poslednú commitnutú verziu). Čo spravíš?
options: git stash pop daného súboru
git restore <súbor>
git commit --amend daného súboru
git reset --hard HEAD na celom repozitári
sk.explain: git restore <súbor> vráti súbor na poslednú commitnutú verziu. reset --hard by zahodil zmeny v CELOM repe.

en.q: In one file you made edits you want to discard entirely (back to the last committed version). What do you do?
options: git stash pop of that file
git restore <file>
git commit --amend of that file
git reset --hard HEAD on the whole repository
en.explain: git restore <file> returns the file to its last committed version. reset --hard would discard changes in the WHOLE repo.

sk.q: Chceš len zistiť, čo je nové na serveri, bez toho aby si si to zlúčil do rozrobenej vetvy. Čo použiješ?
options: git pull
git merge origin/master
git fetch
git push a potom git pull origin master
sk.explain: fetch stiahne novinky bez merge. pull = fetch + merge zodpovedajúcej remote vetvy (origin/<tvoja-vetva>) do tej tvojej — rozrobenú vetvu ti rovno zmení.

en.q: You just want to see what's new on the server without merging it into your work-in-progress branch. What do you use?
options: git pull
git merge origin/master
git fetch
git push and then git pull origin master
en.explain: fetch downloads news without merging. pull = fetch + merge of the matching remote branch (origin/<your-branch>) into yours — it changes your branch right away.

sk.q: Z vetvy feature potrebuješ dostať na Production práve jeden konkrétny commit s opravou. Čo použiješ?
options: git merge feature do vetvy Production
git rebase Production na vetvu feature
git reset Production na daný commit
git cherry-pick <commit>
sk.explain: cherry-pick prenesie JEDEN konkrétny commit. merge/rebase by ťahali celú vetvu, reset by posunul Production inam.

en.q: From the feature branch you need exactly one specific fix commit onto Production. What do you use?
options: git merge feature into the Production branch
git rebase Production onto the feature branch
git reset Production to that commit
git cherry-pick <commit>
en.explain: cherry-pick brings ONE specific commit. merge/rebase would pull the whole branch, reset would move Production elsewhere.

sk.q: Máš rozrobené necommitnuté zmeny, ale musíš hneď prepnúť na master. Nechceš commitnúť polovičnú prácu. Čo spravíš?
options: git stash, prepneš, neskôr git stash pop
git commit -m wip, prepneš a neskôr to celé vrátiš
git reset --hard a prepneš na master
git push rozrobenej vetvy a prepneš na master
sk.explain: stash odloží rozrobené zmeny nabok a vyčistí working tree; po práci ich vrátiš cez stash pop.

en.q: You have uncommitted work-in-progress but must switch to master now. You don't want to commit half-done work. What do you do?
options: git stash, switch, later git stash pop
git commit -m wip, switch and later revert it all
git reset --hard and switch to master
git push the WIP branch and switch to master
en.explain: stash sets the WIP aside and cleans the working tree; after the detour you bring it back with stash pop.

options: Pošle daný súbor na remote server
Pridá zmeny do staging area pre najbližší commit
Vytvorí nový commit s daným súborom
Natrvalo zmaže súbor z histórie repozitára
sk.explain: git add iba stage-uje — vyberie zmeny do staging area. Commit ani push nerobí.

options: Sends the given file to the remote server
Adds the changes to the staging area for the next commit
Creates a new commit with the given file
Permanently deletes the file from the repo's history
en.explain: git add only 'stages' — it picks changes into the staging area. It does not commit or push.

options: Na úplne prvý commit v repozitári
Na aktuálny commit (špičku vetvy)
Na commit o dva commity späť
Na druhú vetvu vytvorenú v repozitári
sk.explain: HEAD~2 je o dva commity späť od aktuálneho (HEAD~1 je rodič, HEAD je teraz).

en.q: What does the notation 'HEAD~2' refer to?
options: The very first commit in the repository
The current commit (the branch tip)
The commit two commits back
The second branch created in the repository
en.explain: HEAD~2 is two commits back from the current one (HEAD~1 is the parent, HEAD is now).

sk.q: Omylom si commitol appsettings.local.json. Pridáš ho do .gitignore — prestane ho Git sledovať?
options: Áno, .gitignore automaticky odstráni aj už commitnuté súbory
Áno, ale prejaví sa to až po ďalšom commite
Nie, najprv treba spraviť git push --force
Nie, treba ho prestať sledovať: git rm --cached
sk.explain: .gitignore platí len na NEsledované súbory. Už commitnutý súbor musíš najprv odstrániť zo sledovania.

en.q: You accidentally committed appsettings.local.json. You add it to .gitignore — will Git stop tracking it?
options: Yes, .gitignore automatically removes already-committed files too
Yes, but it only takes effect after the next commit
No, you must first do a git push --force
No, you must stop tracking it: git rm --cached
en.explain: .gitignore applies only to untracked files. An already-committed file must first be removed from tracking.

sk.q: V KROSe — ako sa tvoja hotová práca dostane do vetvy master?
options: Push, PR, po schválení sa merge-ne do master
Priamym pushom svojich commitov rovno do master
Cherry-pickom konkrétneho commitu do Production
Spravíš git reset vetvy master na svoju lokálnu vetvu
sk.explain: Odvodíš vetvu, pushneš, otvoríš PR; po schválení sa merge-ne do master. Priamy push do master nie je workflow.

en.q: At KROS — how does your finished work get into the master branch?
options: Push, PR, after approval it's merged into master
By pushing your commits straight into master directly
By cherry-picking a specific commit into Production
You git reset the master branch onto your local branch
en.explain: You derive a branch, push, open a PR; after approval it's merged into master. Pushing straight to master isn't the workflow.

sk.q: Cherry-pickol si opravu do Production. Prečo ju používatelia ešte nevidia?
options: Lebo aj cherry-pick treba najprv schváliť cez Pull Request
Production sa musí znova nasadiť — spustí to DevOps master
Lebo zmeny treba pred nasadením ešte raz commitnúť
Nasadenie prebehne úplne automaticky samo do 24 hodín
sk.explain: Cherry-pick zmení vetvu, ale nič nenasadí. Do produkcie sa dostane až manuálnym re-deployom, ktorý spúšťa DevOps master.

en.q: You cherry-picked a fix into Production. Why don't users see it yet?
options: Because even a cherry-pick must first be approved via a Pull Request
Production must be re-deployed — the DevOps master triggers it
Because the changes must be committed once more before deploy
The deployment happens fully automatically by itself within 24 hours
en.explain: A cherry-pick changes the branch but deploys nothing. It reaches production only via a manual re-deploy triggered by the DevOps master.

sk.q: Čo je hlavné riziko príkazu 'git reset --hard HEAD~1'?
options: Pošle nechcené commity rovno na remote
Vytvorí detached HEAD a stratíš vetvu
Zahodí necommitnuté zmeny vo working tree
Natrvalo prepíše históriu na zdieľanom remote serveri
sk.explain: --hard prepíše working tree — necommitnuté zmeny sú nenávratne preč. Preto opatrne.

en.q: What is the main risk of 'git reset --hard HEAD~1'?
options: It pushes unwanted commits straight to the remote
It creates a detached HEAD and you lose the branch
It discards uncommitted changes in the working tree
It permanently rewrites history on the shared remote server
en.explain: --hard overwrites the working tree — uncommitted changes are gone for good. So use it carefully.

sk.q: Ktorý prístup spraví históriu lineárnou (akoby si robil od začiatku nad aktuálnym master)?
options: Merge, ktorý vytvorí spoločný merge commit
git pull spustený bez akýchkoľvek argumentov
Cherry-pick všetkých commitov po jednom
Rebase vetvy nad master
sk.explain: Rebase prenesie commity nad špičku master a história ostane lineárna. Merge naopak pridá merge commit.

en.q: Which approach makes the history linear (as if you'd worked on the current master from the start)?
options: A merge that creates a shared merge commit
git pull run without any arguments at all
Cherry-picking all the commits one by one
Rebasing the branch onto master
en.explain: Rebase replays commits on top of master's tip and keeps history linear. A merge instead adds a merge commit.

sk.q: Tvoja vetva je na remote a pracujú na nej aj kolegovia. Prečo ju nerebase-ovať?
options: Rebase prepíše ID commitov a rozbije kópie kolegom
Rebase je citeľne pomalší ako bežný merge
Rebase sa principiálne nedá spustiť na remote vetve
Rebase natrvalo vymaže celú vetvu aj s jej históriou
sk.explain: Rebase prepisuje históriu (nové ID commitov). Na zdieľanej vetve tým rozbiješ kópie ostatným.

en.q: Your branch is on the remote and colleagues work on it too. Why not rebase it?
options: Rebase rewrites commit IDs and breaks colleagues' copies
Rebase is noticeably slower than a regular merge
Rebase fundamentally cannot be run on a remote branch
Rebase permanently deletes the whole branch and its history
en.explain: Rebase rewrites history (new commit IDs). On a shared branch that breaks everyone else's copy.

sk.q: Spravil si commit, no kolega tvoje zmeny stále nevidí. Prečo?
options: Commit zlyhal kvôli pravidlu v .gitignore
Commit je lokálny — chýba git push
Treba spraviť git fetch namiesto commitu
Zmeny sú stále len v staging area, nie v commite
sk.explain: Commit ide len do lokálnej histórie. Kým nespravíš push, na remote (a teda u kolegu) zmeny nie sú.

en.q: You made a commit, but your colleague still doesn't see your changes. Why?
options: The commit failed because of a .gitignore rule
The commit is local — git push is missing
You need to run git fetch instead of committing
The changes are still only in the staging area, not in a commit
en.explain: A commit goes only into local history. Until you push, the changes aren't on the remote (so not at the colleague).

sk.q: Prečo sú git worktrees užitočné pri práci s AI agentom?
options: Zrýchlia samotné commitovanie a pushovanie zmien
Automaticky schvaľujú otvorené Pull Requesty
Umožnia paralelnú prácu na viacerých featúrach naraz
Zálohujú celý lokálny repozitár na vzdialený server
sk.explain: Každý worktree je iná vetva v inom priečinku — agent môže bežať na viacerých featúrach paralelne bez prepínania.

en.q: Why are git worktrees useful when working with an AI agent?
options: They speed up committing and pushing changes themselves
They automatically approve open Pull Requests
They allow parallel work on several features at once
They back up the whole local repository to a remote server
en.explain: Each worktree is a different branch in a different folder — the agent can work on several features in parallel without switching.

sk.q: Checkoutol si konkrétny commit (nie vetvu) a commitol tam. Si v detached HEAD a chceš prácu zachovať. Čo spravíš?
options: git reset --hard HEAD na aktuálnom mieste
git push origin HEAD priamo z detached stavu
git stash a následne prepneš späť na master
git switch -c nova-vetva
sk.explain: Založením vetvy z aktuálneho miesta (git switch -c) zachytíš commity, ktoré by sa inak v detached HEAD stratili.

en.q: You checked out a specific commit (not a branch) and committed there. You're in detached HEAD and want to keep the work. What do you do?
options: git reset --hard HEAD at the current location
git push origin HEAD straight from the detached state
git stash and then switch back to master
git switch -c new-branch
en.explain: Creating a branch from the current spot (git switch -c) captures the commits that would otherwise be lost in detached HEAD.
